Check the agent log on this host and consider restarting the sysmanage-agent service. Click Refresh / Install Tools again to retry — the in-flight marker will be cleared and a fresh attempt dispatched.

enroll.title: Enroll a federation site
enroll.tokenInstructions: Copy the enrollment token below and deliver it to the new site server. This is the only time it will be displayed — there is no recovery if lost.
enroll.siteIdentityKeyHelp: Required. Paste the site's identity public key, obtained out of band (from the site's Server Role page). The coordinator verifies the site's enrollment proof against this key — it is what defeats an enrollment-time man-in-the-middle.

enterpriseRequired.body: Federation lets you manage many SysManage servers from one coordinator. Upgrade to an Enterprise license to enroll subordinate sites here.
enterpriseRequired.title: Multi-site federation requires Enterprise

sites.subtitle: Subordinate SysManage servers enrolled with this coordinator.
sites.title: Federation Sites

sitesMap.subtitle: Geographic distribution of subordinate site servers.
sitesMap.title: Federation Sites Map

airgapCollections.title: Air-Gap Collection Runs
airgapCollections.subtitle: Trigger one-shot collection runs that mirror configured repositories, build a signed ISO, and write a manifest for ingestion on the air-gapped side.
airgapCollections.notApplicable: This page is only meaningful on collector-role deployments.
airgapCollections.licenseRequired: Air-gap collector engine not loaded; Pro+ license required.
airgapCollections.empty: No collection runs yet. Click "New Collection Run" to start one.
airgapCollections.confirmDelete: Delete this collection run and its manifests?
airgapCollections.noManifestFound: Run has no manifest yet — try again once status is COMPLETE.

dialog.title: New Collection Run
dialog.isoLabelHelper: Short identifier embedded in the produced ISO (e.g. "monthly-2026-05").
dialog.mediaSizeHelper: Maximum size per disc in MB. Defaults to 4700 (DVD-5).
dialog.mirrorsRequired: At least one mirror target is required.
dialog.hostMismatch: All picked mirrors must live on the same host.
dialog.mirrorsHelper: Pick one or more configured mirrors. The bundle is built from a fresh snapshot of each mirror tree taken at run creation. All picks must share a host.
dialog.noMirrors: No enabled mirrors configured. Go to Settings → Repository Mirroring to add one, then try again.
dialog.hostMismatchWarning: The mirrors you picked span multiple hosts. The collection plan dispatches to a single host, so all picks must share one.
dialog.burnDeviceHelper: Leave blank to build a downloadable ISO file only. Setting a device adds a BURNING stage after ISO_BUILT.

airgapCollections.noIsoOnDisk: No ISO file found on disk yet — re-poll in a moment.
airgapCollections.multiDiscInfo: This run produced {{count}} discs. Pick which to download.

airgapBundles.confirmDelete: Delete this bundle and its ISO file?
airgapBundles.dockerInstallHint: Run this on the sysmanage server as a user with sudo:
airgapBundles.dockerNoGroup: Docker is running but the {{user}} user is not in the docker group, so the build subprocess cannot reach the daemon socket.
airgapBundles.dockerNotInstalled: Docker is not installed on this server. Bundle builds need Docker to fetch per-distro Linux packages.
airgapBundles.dockerNotRunning: Docker is installed but the daemon is not reachable. Start it with: sudo systemctl enable --now docker.
airgapBundles.dockerPermissionDenied: The Docker daemon is running but the {{user}} user (the one running this sysmanage process) cannot read /var/run/docker.sock. Add the user to the docker group, then restart the sysmanage process so the new group set takes effect.
airgapBundles.dockerReadyHint: Builds typically take 5-30 minutes depending on how many platforms are enabled.
airgapBundles.empty: No bundles yet. Click a build button above to create one.
airgapBundles.requiresDocker: Bundle builds require Docker to be installed and running on this server — needed to fetch per-distro Linux packages. Builds typically take 5-30 minutes depending on how many platforms are enabled.
airgapBundles.subtitle: Generate a multi-OS ISO containing sysmanage server or agent + all per-platform dependencies, ready to mount on an air-gapped host.
airgapBundles.title: Air-Gap Install Bundles
airgapBundles.resourcesInsufficient: This server does not have enough free memory or disk to build a Server or Agent bundle — those buttons are disabled until resources are freed (add swap or grow the VM).
airgapBundles.resourcesLow: Low resources — the build can run but will lean on swap and may be slow.

serverRole.loadError: Could not load the current server role.
serverRole.saveError: Could not save the server role.

standard.title: Standard (no air gap)
standard.description: The default. This server manages hosts directly over the network with no air-gap separation. Choose this for any ordinary deployment — it is what every server runs as unless you specifically need one half of an air-gapped pair.

collector.title: Air-Gap Collector (online side)
collector.description: The internet-connected half of an air-gap pair. It mirrors upstream package repositories, gathers CVE and compliance feeds, and bundles them into signed ISO images that you physically carry across the air gap. Choose this on the server that has internet access and builds the media.

repository.title: Air-Gap Repository (disconnected side)
repository.description: The isolated half of an air-gap pair, with no internet access. It ingests the ISO media produced by the Collector, verifies its signature, and serves the packages to managed hosts on the private network so they can update normally. Choose this on the server inside the air-gapped enclave.

serverRole.heading: Air-Gap Role
serverRole.intro: Choose how this SysManage server participates in an air-gapped deployment. Most servers should stay on Standard. The role takes effect after the next server restart.
serverRole.restartHint: Restart the SysManage server after saving for the new role to take full effect.
serverRole.saved: Server role saved. Restart the server for it to take full effect.

airgapDevices.empty: No eligible drives found. Insert media and press Rescan.
airgapDevices.help: Pick the optical/USB drive that holds the collector media. The Import button on the Air-Gap Repositories page becomes available when this drive has a readable ISO inserted. The media signature is verified during import.
airgapDevices.label: Block device
airgapDevices.title: Import Drive

airgapImport.noDevice: No import drive selected. Choose one in Settings → Server Role.
airgapImport.statusError: Could not probe the import device.
airgapImport.title: Import from Media
airgapImport.runDone: Import complete — the repository list below is refreshed.

collector.help: Copy this public key and import it on the Air-Gap Repository server so it will trust the signed media this collector produces. Only the public key is shown — the private signing key never leaves this server.
collector.loadError: Could not load the collector public key.
collector.title: Collector Public Key

trusted.help: Import the public key from each Air-Gap Collector whose media this repository should accept. Media signed by an untrusted key is rejected during ingest.
trusted.required: Name and public key are both required.
trusted.title: Trusted Collector Keys

detail.noLiveLink: Live detail link unavailable (owning site has no URL).
detail.notFound: This host is no longer in the directory.
detail.title: Host detail

federationHosts.empty: No hosts have been synced from any site yet.
federationHosts.emptyFiltered: No hosts match the current filters.
federationHosts.subtitle: Every host across all enrolled sites, from the coordinator's synced directory.
federationHosts.title: Cross-Site Hosts

errors.packagesRequired: Enter at least one package name.

federationDispatch.hostIdsHint: Comma-separated host IDs (from the Hosts page)
federationDispatch.subtitle: Queue a command for {{site}}. The site runs it on its hosts and reports results back — nothing executes immediately.
federationDispatch.title: Dispatch command
federationDispatch.subtitleMulti: Queue a command for {{hosts}} selected host(s) across {{sites}} site(s). Each site runs it and reports results back — nothing executes immediately.

federationAlertConfig.subtitle: Leave a field blank to use the built-in default.
federationAlertConfig.title: Alert thresholds

federationReport.subtitle: Aggregate host, compliance, and vulnerability rollups across sites.

coordinator.description: The hub that aggregates host inventory, compliance, and vulnerability rollups from many subordinate site servers and dispatches commands and policies down to them. One coordinator per federation.
coordinator.title: Federation Coordinator

federationRole.heading: Federation Role

identity.help: Copy this public key and paste it into the peer's federation card so each side can record the other's identity fingerprint and verify it out of band. The private key never leaves this server.
identity.title: This server's identity key

federationRole.intro: Choose how this server participates in a multi-site federation. This is independent of the air-gap role on the left — a server can be both. Takes effect after the next restart.
federationRole.loadError: Could not load the federation role.

none.description: The default. This server is not part of a multi-site federation. Choose this unless you are running a coordinator or a subordinate site.
none.title: Not federated

peers.empty: No peer identity keys recorded yet.
peers.help: Record a peer's public key here to verify its identity fingerprint out of band. The federation connection itself is secured by the TLS certificate pinned during enrollment — these keys are for human verification, not access control.
peers.name: Peer name
peers.title: Peer identity keys

federationRole.saveError: Could not save the federation role.
federationRole.saved: Federation role saved. Restart the server for it to take full effect.

site.description: A subordinate server that runs autonomously and reports up to a coordinator. It keeps managing its own hosts even when the coordinator is unreachable, replaying queued data on reconnect.
site.title: Federation Site

enroll.error: Could not enroll with the coordinator.
enroll.help: Point this site at its coordinator and paste the one-time enrollment token the coordinator issued (from its Sites page). This site calls back to complete the handshake and begin syncing.
enroll.notEnrolled: Not yet enrolled with a coordinator.
enroll.title: Enroll with coordinator
enroll.coordIdentityKeyHelp: Required. Paste the coordinator's identity public key, obtained out of band (from its Server Role page). This site refuses to enroll unless the coordinator proves this exact key — it is what defeats an enrollment-time man-in-the-middle.

configuration.description: Server-wide operational settings. Saved values are stored in the database and override sysmanage.yaml.
configuration.secretSetHelp: A password is stored in OpenBAO. Type a new one to replace it.
configuration.secretUnsetHelp: Stored securely in OpenBAO, not in the database.
configuration.title: Configuration
